title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Go Semantics Case: Authn Proof Principal Session
summary: A service invents password hashing, trusts JWT payloads before verification, accepts any algorithm and audience, stores long-lived bearer tokens in logs, and leaves sessions valid after recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo authn proof principal session increment in authentication and credential lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
Model PATCH semantics, missing versus explicit zero or null, immutable and server-owned fields, property authorization, atomic validation, and response evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
